Following are the official attend
ance figures for the week, as com
piled by Mr.. Jones:


Monday, 1852; Tuesday, 1850;
Wednesday, 1880; Thursday, co.,
021; Friday, 1920.


Added to the total attendance
thus far, Mr.. Jones estimate Of
10000 for today will bring the at
tendance to approximately 10000


Early in the week the trustees
expressed themselves as pleased
If the total attendance reached
100000. This number will be ex-
ceeded. which is a matter of much
satisfaction to those who have
worked for the success of what is
admitted by publicity experts ia
be the greatest and most far-reach.
ing undertaking from a standpoint
of publicity, ever undertaken in
the northwest, except the Lewis &
Clark exposition, and the greatest
for the amount of money expend-
ed. There was no government,
state or county aid. 1t was all a
made in Spokane" project, and all
the bills were footed by private
donation.


I. A. Nadeau, director general of
the A-Y-P exposition, arrived from
Seattle this morning.


A special train carrying about
100 Portland people arrived at
1880 and were rendered break.
fast at 8 o'clock at the Silver Grill.
